title: Chinese Remainder Theorem
description: Solve systems of congruences using CRT
mermaid: graph TD A1[Input System of Congruences: x ≡ a1 (mod n1) x ≡ a2 (mod n2) ... x ≡ ak (mod nk)] style A1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff B1["Initialize: N = n1 * n2 * ... * nk"] style B1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000 C1["For i = 1 to k"] style C1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000 D1["Calculate: Ni = N / ni"] style D1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff E1["Find the modular inverse: yi such that Ni * yi ≡ 1 (mod ni)"] style E1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff F1["Is yi found?"] style F1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000 G1["Compute: xi = ai * Ni * yi"] style G1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff H1["Add xi to the sum: Sum = Sum + xi"] style H1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff I1["Next i"] style I1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000 J1["Compute the final solution: x ≡ Sum (mod N)"] style J1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff K1["Simplify x to the smallest positive integer"] style K1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff L1[Output: Solution x] style L1 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff M1[Error: Modular inverse does not exist. n1, n2, ..., nk may not be pairwise coprime.] style M1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff A1 --> B1 B1 --> C1 C1 --> D1 D1 --> E1 E1 --> F1 F1 -- Yes --> G1 F1 -- No --> M1 G1 --> H1 H1 --> I1 I1 --> C1 C1 -- i > k --> J1 J1 --> K1 K1 --> L1
entities: Input System of Congruences: x ≡ a1 (mod n1) x ≡ a2 (mod n2) ... x ≡ ak (mod nk)
"Find the modular inverse: yi such that Ni * yi ≡ 1 (mod ni)"
"Add xi to the sum: Sum = Sum + xi"
"Compute the final solution: x ≡ Sum (mod N)"
"Simplify x to the smallest positive integer"
Error: Modular inverse does not exist. n1, n2, ..., nk may not be pairwise coprime.
